_doc:
Source-of-truth classification for every Brixies data-layer the parser encounters. The import script (scripts/import-brixies-catalog.mjs) consumes this file. Edit by hand as new families land — scripts/inventory-brixies-layers.mjs surfaces any layer name the taxonomy hasn't classified yet.

v2 (2026-05-13) covers the full 18-family Brixies HTML drop. Bedrock-pass design decisions:

  - Section kind enum: content | chrome | functional | media | embed | component | post_template (see `kinds`).
  - Embed blocks are a first-class page block type — NOT a Brixies template. Strategist or AI inserts via the page composer; renders as a tagged placeholder card. See `embed_block_categories`.
  - Card palette: 2-4 Card N variants chosen per project at brand-design phase, amendable at content phase. Listings and feature sections reference 'from_palette' as their item shape rather than hardcoding a Card N id. See `card_palette`.
  - Post template auto-pair: when a listing template (Team/Blog/Career/Event/Sermon) is placed on a page AND the display mode is 'wordpress' (Option 3 of the sermons-events-groups rule), the paired single-* template auto-adds a detail page to the sitemap. Options 1 (archive link) and 2 (ChMS embed) do not trigger auto-pair. See `post_template_pairs`.
  - Heading levels (H1-H6) preserved as a `level` property on the heading slot so the writing rules' 'one H1 per page' invariant can be enforced. See `heading_levels`.

_meta.coverage_target: ≥98% post-v2.2; remaining unmapped layers route to 'unknown text slot' fallback for surfacing in the discovery report.
notes: Brixies inconsistently outputs `Feature Section` (caps) and `Feature section` (lower) for the same family. Parser must treat family detection AND group-container detection as case-insensitive (see parser_config).

59 standalone Card N templates (Card 100, Card 124, etc.) live as `component`-kind entries, not section variants. Consumed by sections that need repeating-item shapes, drawn from the project's card_palette.
Single Event / Single Team / Single Post are `post_template`-kind. They auto-pair with their listing-template counterparts only when display mode = 'wordpress'.
Content Sections export was re-saved 2026-05-13 because the original Brixies file was missing the parent wrapper layer name — re-run the inventory if upstream Brixies fixes the export.

STRUCTURAL POSTURE: templates are classified by their structural parts (layout, slot/group shape) and NOT by their original family's use-case. A 'pricing card' layout can be repurposed for a staff callout or event highlight — the Content Manager filters by structure, not by family. Avoid scoping wrappers / containers to specific families in the taxonomy.

Inventory's family detection over-eagerly flagged false section roots (Frame N, Image N, Service N, Rectangle N, Button Group N). These are now constrained by `families_allowed` — the parser only treats a `<Family> N` layer as a section root if Family is in that allow-list.

parser_config._doc: Behavior flags consumed by the parser. Allows us to adjust matching strictness without re-touching individual rules.

ignored_layer_patterns._doc:
Layer names the parser skips entirely — neither slot, nor wrapper, nor decoration, nor group. Used for Brixies-internal artifacts: placeholder SVG wrappers (Logoipsum logos), lorem-ipsum text values accidentally authored as `data-layer` attributes by the designer, and Brixies design tokens that have no semantic value in the output schema. Logged once at inventory time; silently dropped at import time.

Grouped by reason:
  - Brixies placeholder wrappers: Logoipsum, circle-play-solid, logo-N
  - Lorem ipsum text placeholders
  - Menu-link label placeholders (HOME, ABOUT US, BLOG, etc.) — actual nav authoring happens in the sitemap editor, not at section-import time. Parser surfaces these to the sitemap editor with the source template id so the user can wire label+url pairs there.
  - Sign-in / Register UI (out of scope for church sites in current phase)
  - Date subcomponents (Year, Month & Year, Day date/week alone) — composed at render time from a single Date slot (see slot config).
  - First/Second/Third/Fourth link generic enumerators
  - Phone / address placeholder values — see global_site_snippets for the merge-field resolution path

kinds._doc: Top-level section-type enum. `section_kind_rules` maps section root layer names to one of these values; the catalog's `kind` column stores it; the Content Manager's 'Add Section' picker filters by it.

descriptions.content: Hand-authored body sections — Hero, Feature, FAQ, Intro, Process, Timeline, Blog (when authored), CTA, Banner.
descriptions.chrome: Site frame, not page-specific — Header, Footer, Megamenu, Offcanvas, top-of-page banner bars.
descriptions.functional: Interactive UI primitives — Filter, Search, Pagination, Sort, Category Filter, Tag Filter.
descriptions.media: Video / audio / gallery-led sections — Gallery Section, sermon player, hero with video bg.
descriptions.embed: First-class page block type — NOT a Brixies template. See embed_block_categories.
descriptions.component: Reusable card library (the 59 Card N variants). Not orderable as a page section on its own; consumed by other sections via the project's card_palette.
descriptions.post_template: Single-entity detail pages — Single Event, Single Team, Single Post, Single Career, Single Sermon. Auto-pair to a listing template under Option 3 (Managed in WordPress).

embed_block_categories._doc:
First-class page block type. Not a Brixies section. Strategist or AI inserts via the page composer; the wireframe renderer shows a tagged placeholder card (category + title + what's-included). At build time, the developer replaces the placeholder with the actual iframe / feed / widget code.

The AI copywriter does NOT draft body content for embed blocks — only the `title` and `whats_included` fields. The placeholder is a deferred-content marker.

category_notes.event: Single event registrations, full month-view calendars, or featured upcoming events. Source typically Church Center / PCO Registrations / TicketSpice.
category_notes.forms: Contact, prayer-request, newsletter signup, volunteer interest. Mailchimp, ConvertKit, Tally, embedded ChMS forms.
category_notes.groups: PCO Groups feed, Church Center group browser.
category_notes.instagram: Instagram feed grid widgets (Smash Balloon, EmbedSocial).
category_notes.maps: Google / Mapbox embed for service location, campus map.
category_notes.prayer: Embedded prayer-wall, prayer-request form distinct from generic contact form.
category_notes.sermon: Sermon archive widgets — Subsplash, Church Center, ChurchPlugins. May overlap with youtube_playlist when YouTube is the source.
category_notes.youtube_playlist: Full YouTube playlist embed for any purpose (sermons, testimonies, kids content). Distinct from latest-sermons because it's a full archive view.

title.description: Section heading shown above the placeholder (e.g., 'Latest Sermons').

whats_included.description: What content shows up here at build time (e.g., 'The 3 most recent sermon videos with title, date, watch button').

source_url.description: Link to existing content if client already has it elsewhere (YouTube channel, current site page).

embed_code.description: Raw <iframe> or widget code if client supplied it directly.

source_notes.description: Developer notes — e.g., 'pull from current site footer once dev access granted', 'awaiting Tithely API key from finance team'.

card_palette._doc:
Project-level cohesion mechanism. A `web_projects.card_palette` field holds 2-4 Card N template ids — the visual card shapes used across the entire site. Listings and feature sections that need a repeating-item shape reference 'from_palette' rather than hardcoding a Card N id; the renderer + Figma plugin + WordPress importer all read the project's palette to know which card to render in any given slot.

Net effect: visual cohesion across the site (the same 2-4 card shapes recur everywhere) without sections being locked to one specific card variant.
card_palette.schema_location: web_projects.card_palette (jsonb array of Card N template ids, length 2-4)
card_palette.amendment_trigger: If a section's content needs don't fit any palette card during content phase, the strategist can add a new card to the palette (up to the 4-card cap) or swap one out. Triggered by an explicit 'Expand palette' action in the Content Manager; not automatic.

post_template_pairs._doc:
Listing template family -> paired single-* template family. When a listing template is placed on a page AND the display_mode is 'wordpress' (Option 3 of the sermons-events-groups rule), the paired single-* template auto-adds a detail page to the sitemap with a slug pattern like `/<entity>/[slug]`.

Options 1 (archive link) and 2 (ChMS embed) do NOT trigger auto-pair because the detail view is hosted externally (YouTube, Church Center, etc.) — no detail page needed on our site.

Strategist can manually remove the auto-added detail page if it isn't wanted; it's a default-on convenience, not a hard requirement.

chrome_auto_populated_fields._doc:
Footer / header slots that are auto-populated from project-level metadata rather than authored per-page. Strategist doesn't see these in the section editor — they're filled at build time from `web_projects` fields. Keeps cookie disclosures, credit lines, and legal notices consistent across every page without the strategist having to copy them.

Add a new entry here when a layer that recurs across chrome families is truly chrome-level rather than per-page authored.

global_site_snippets._doc:
Project-level data points that resolve via merge field tokens in copy. Available everywhere body content is authored. The AI copywriter inserts these tokens where intake confirms a real value exists (e.g. an address block on a Contact section). The renderer substitutes the merge field with the project's value at build time.

Distinct from `chrome_auto_populated_fields`:
  - chrome_auto_populated_fields = chrome-only slots (footer cookies, legal). Strategist never sees them.
  - global_site_snippets = merge fields available IN ANY body slot. Strategist + AI copywriter can reference them.

Distinct from `embed_block_categories`:
  - embed = third-party widget placeholder (sermon archive, Instagram feed). Rendered as a placeholder block.
  - global_site_snippets = inline text values (church name, phone, address). Substituted into authored copy.

Brixies placeholder values that resemble snippet content (e.g. '+31 987 654 321', 'Calle Lorem ipsum N° 158 Av. Roma Spagna') are matched by `placeholder_resolution_patterns` below. Matching layers are dropped at parse time; the AI copywriter is expected to insert the merge token in the slot when intake indicates the value is set on the project.

families_allowed._doc:
Allow-list of real Brixies section-root family names. The parser only treats a `<Family> N` layer as a section root if Family appears here (case-insensitive). Constrains the inventory's over-eager family detection that flags Frame / Image / Service / Rectangle / Button Group as false roots.

Family names are CANONICAL — `Feature Section` covers both `Feature Section N` and `Feature section N` (case-insensitive match per parser_config).

Add entries when a new Brixies family ships.

chrome_designation._doc:
Header / Footer / Megamenu / Offcanvas templates ship at `kind: 'chrome'` and live in the catalog like any other template. Per-project, the user designates which chrome templates apply via the sitemap editor:

  - PRIMARY HEADER (one per project) — auto-renders on every page above the page content.
  - PRIMARY FOOTER (one per project) — auto-renders on every page below the page content.
  - MEGAMENU SECTIONS (zero or more) — alternative nav variants. Render only in the style-guide-for-dev export, not on actual pages.
  - OFFCANVAS SECTIONS (zero or more) — same as Megamenu — style-guide reference for dev only.

When a Header / Footer template is parsed, the strategist authors nav items in the sitemap editor (label + URL pairs per the writing-rules 'every CTA carries its URL' rule). Brixies placeholder labels (HOME, ABOUT US, BLOG) are ignored at parse time; the sitemap editor surfaces fresh nav entry rows.

Schema implications (for v28 migration):
  - web_projects.primary_header_template_id (uuid, fk to web_content_templates, nullable)
  - web_projects.primary_footer_template_id (uuid, fk to web_content_templates, nullable)
  - web_projects.megamenu_template_ids (uuid[], optional)
  - web_projects.offcanvas_template_ids (uuid[], optional)
  - web_projects.nav_items (jsonb) — authored array of { label, url, parent_id? }

heading_levels._doc:
Brixies labels heading slots with explicit levels: `Heading`, `Heading H2`, `Heading H3`, etc. The parser must preserve the level as a property on the slot so the writing rules' 'one H1 per page' invariant can be enforced.

A bare `Heading` defaults to level 2 inside sections; the level-1 H1 belongs to the page's hero. Hero templates override the default to level 1 on their root heading.

slots._doc:
data-layer name → slot field schema. A slot is a single fixed position in the layout — the strategist authors one value. Only entries here render with typed inputs in the editor; everything else falls through to the structural / decoration / unknown classifier.

Grouped logically below: core text, headings-by-level, descriptions, images, dates, authors, taxonomies/tags, content, counters, social, post-detail-page slots, form inputs.

Excerpt.description: Short preview of post/event content, used on listing cards.

Img.description: Alternate name Brixies uses for some image slots.

Featured Image.label: Featured image
Featured Image.description: Lead image on a detail page (post / event / team).

Map.label: Map location
Map.description: Address or lat/long pin for an event venue or church location.

Date.label: Date
Date.description: The single authored date value. Renderer composes Day date / Day week / Month & Year subcomponents from this; strategist doesn't author the parts separately (see ignored_layer_patterns for Year / Month & Year, decorations for Day date / Day week).

Content.label: Body content
Content.description: Full body content of a post / event / team detail page. Supports headings, lists, quotes, embedded images.

Content with bold.description: Body paragraph with inline bold runs. Renders the same as Content; named separately for designer reference.

Counter number.label: Stat number
Counter number.description: The big number on a stat counter card. Allows formatting like '150k+', '2,000', '500+'.

Counter description.description: Alt-form description used by some Counter variants. Most Counters use the global `Description` slot for body copy; this entry covers the variants that named it 'Counter description' instead.

Alert.label: Alert message
Alert.description: Banner / alert content (e.g., 'Service times updated for the holiday week').

Menu Item.label: Nav link
Menu Item.description: Header / Megamenu navigation item. Carries both the label text and the destination URL (same shape as a button CTA). Strategist authors both, per the writing rules' 'every CTA carries its destination URL' rule.

Location.label: Address or place name
Location.description: Street address or venue / place name. Distinct from `Map` (the visual pin). When the value is a church address, the AI copywriter prefers the `{{church_address}}` merge field.

Subheading.description: Sub-headline beneath the main heading. Smaller than Heading, more emphatic than Tagline.

Short description.description: Compressed body copy — used on cards / listings / hero variants where space is tight.

Accent description.description: Description with visual accent treatment (often pull-quote style).

Position.label: Role / title
Position.description: Alt-name for Team position. Some Brixies templates label this slot 'Position' instead of 'Team position'.

Tag.label: Tag / label
Tag.description: Generic tag chip — category, status, theme. May appear in card grids, post archives, filter rows.

View all.label: 'View all' link
View all.description: Link to the full archive (e.g., 'View all posts').

Value.label: Value
Value.description: Alt-naming for a counter value or stat number when the template doesn't use 'Counter number'.

Video link.label: Video link CTA
Video link.description: URL + label for a play-video button — typically opens an embedded player or modal.

Breadcrumbs.label: Breadcrumb trail
Breadcrumbs.description: E.g., 'Home / Events / Easter Sunday'. Auto-generated from the page hierarchy in most cases; this slot allows manual override.

group_container_hints._doc: Layer names known to be card / repeating-group containers. The parser detects groups structurally (2+ siblings of the same data-layer at the same depth) — this list is a fallback for single-instance card-shaped containers that should be treated as groups even when only one is in the source. Add entries when a new family ships a card variant that's only shown once in the starter template.

decorations._doc: Layer-name patterns (regex strings, case-insensitive) for decorative SVG / icons / shapes. These are visual decoration the strategist never authors — silently skipped by the parser. Add new patterns when the discovery report surfaces them.
patterns: ^Mask gradient( top| bot| left| right| reference)?$

wrappers._doc: Layer-name patterns for layout wrappers. These are not authored, not decorated — purely structural. Children of a wrapper still get walked. Add patterns when the discovery report surfaces a new wrapper.

section_kind_rules._doc: Maps a section root layer name to a kind value (see `kinds`). Rules are evaluated top-down; first match wins. Case-insensitive matching is REQUIRED — Brixies inconsistently outputs e.g. `Feature Section` vs `Feature section`. Add new rules as families land.

rules.pattern: ^(Single Event|Single Team|Single Post|Single Career|Single Sermon) Section( \d+)?$

kind_notes.embed: NOT routed via section_kind_rules — embed blocks are not Brixies sections. See embed_block_categories for the page-block flow.
kind_notes.media_pending: No families currently route to `media`. Reserved for future video-player / audio-player templates whose primary content is media. Gallery Sections route to `content` (a Gallery is authored body content, not a media-led layout).
kind_notes.ui_labels: Display labels are localized in the UI layer — `chrome` shows as 'Global' (since chrome sections render on every page) and `functional` shows as 'Filter'. Schema values stay canonical.

scoped_key_rules._doc: When a slot layer (e.g. Heading) appears under one of these parents, the emitted key is suffixed with the parent's slug instead of using the bare slot key. Prevents Heading-as-filter-label from clobbering Heading-as-section-H1. Parser still applies its own structural detection — these are the named exceptions where scoping is most useful.
